entities.name: RICE
entities.description: Upland rice cultivars evaluated for phosphorus response and efficiency

entities.name: GRAIN YIELD
entities.description: The amount of grain produced per unit area, which increased with phosphorus application

entities.name: PHOSPHORUS EFFICIENCY
entities.description: The ability of rice cultivars to utilize phosphorus for growth and yield

entities.name: AGRONOMIC EFFICIENCY
entities.description: A measure of phosphorus efficiency based on yield increase per unit of phosphorus applied

entities.name: PHYSIOLOGICAL EFFICIENCY
entities.description: A measure of phosphorus efficiency based on yield increase per unit of phosphorus absorbed

entities.name: PHOSPHORUS HARVEST INDEX
entities.description: The ratio of phosphorus in the grain to the total phosphorus in the plant

entities.name: INTERNAL EFFICIENCY OF PHOSPHORUS
entities.description: The efficiency with which the plant uses the phosphorus it has absorbed, determining overall P efficiency

entities.name: FIELD EXPERIMENTS
entities.description: Three-year study (1993-1995) conducted to determine rice response to phosphorus and its residues

entities.name: PHOSPHORUS DEFICIENCY
entities.description: A major limiting factor for crop production in highly weathered soils of West Africa

entities.name: LOW AVAILABLE PHOSPHORUS
entities.description: The specific nutrient status of the Ultisol soil at the experimental site

entities.name: FERTILIZER PHOSPHORUS APPLICATION
entities.description: The application of phosphorus fertilizer at rates of 0, 45, 90, 145, and 180 kg P ha-1

entities.name: WEST AFRICA
entities.description: The broad geographic region characterized by highly weathered soils in the humid zone

entities.name: IVORY COAST
entities.description: The specific country in West Africa where the field experiments were conducted

entities.name: ULTISOL
entities.description: A type of highly weathered soil found in the humid forest zone of Ivory Coast

entities.name: RESIDUAL VALUE OF FERTILIZER PHOSPHORUS
entities.description: The continuing effect of phosphorus fertilizer applied in a previous year on subsequent crops
